steps.title: Set up the angle sum equation using the isosceles condition
steps.content:
In any triangle, the three angles sum to 180°. Since ∠J = ∠K, let both equal $x$. Then:
$$x + x + 136° = 180°$$
$$2x + 136° = 180°$$

steps.title: Solve for the measure of ∠J
steps.content:
Subtract 136° from both sides:
$$2x = 44°$$
$$x = 22°$$
Since ∠J = $x$, the measure of ∠J is 22°. That matches choice A — A is correct.
